name: Nougat (ML PDF to Markdown Converter)

auth.notes: Local ML model; no auth required

performance.notes: Vision transformer inference; GPU recommended; 1-5 pages/min on GPU

agent_readiness.notes: Vision transformer model for converting academic PDFs to markdown. from nougat import NougatModel. model = NougatModel.from_pretrained('facebook/nougat-base'). nougat path/to/paper.pdf -o output/ (CLI). Handles: LaTeX equations (converted to markdown math), tables, figures, complex layouts. Trained on academic papers. Slower than marker-pdf but better equation handling. GPU strongly recommended. Batch processing via CLI. Output: .mmd files (mathpix markdown).

gotchas: Slow without GPU (5-10x slower on CPU)
Trained on academic papers — struggles with non-academic PDFs
Model size (~1GB) requires download on first use
best_when: Converting academic/scientific PDFs with LaTeX equations to markdown
Research paper ingestion pipelines requiring math notation preservation
When marker-pdf's equation handling is insufficient
Batch conversion of arXiv papers for LLM training data
avoid_when: General business PDFs (marker-pdf or unstructured.io better)
Real-time PDF processing (too slow without GPU cluster)
When equations are not present (marker-pdf faster and better)
summary: Nougat is Meta's vision transformer for converting academic PDFs to markdown with LaTeX equation preservation. CLI: nougat paper.pdf -o output/. Output: .mmd (mathpix markdown) with math blocks. Best for scientific papers from arXiv with complex equations. GPU required for reasonable speed. Slower than marker-pdf but better at math notation. For general PDFs, use marker-pdf or pymupdf.
